verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus therefore said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except ye eat the flesh of the Son of Man, and drink His blood, ye have not eternal life in yourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whoso eateth My flesh, and drinketh My blood, hath life in himself.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When we converse of spiritual things, let there be nothing secular in our souls, nothing earthy, let all such thoughts retire, and be banished, and let us be entirely given up to the hearing the divine oracles only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if at the arrival of a king all confusion is driven away, much more when the Spirit speaketh with us do we need great stillness, great awe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And worthy of awe is that which is said to-day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How it is so, hear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Verily I say unto you, Except a man eat My flesh, and drink My blood, he hath not eternal life in him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since the Jews had before asserted that this was impossible, He showeth not only that it is not impossible, but that it is absolutely necessary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He addeth, “He that eateth My flesh and drinketh My blood, hath eternal life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And I will raise him up at the last day.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since He had said, “He that eateth of this bread shall not die for ever” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 50 , not verbally quoted), and it was likely that this would stand in their way, (just as they before said, “Abraham is dead, and the prophets are dead; and how sayest Thou, that he shall not taste of death?”— c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 52 , not verbally quoted.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He bringeth forward the Resurrection to solve the question, and to show that (the man who eateth) shall not die at the last.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He continually handleth the subject of the Mysteries, showing the necessity of the action, and that it must by all means be done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For My flesh is true meat, and My blood is true drink.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is that He saith?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He either desireth to declare that this is the true meat which saveth the soul, or to assure them concerning what had been said, that they might not suppose the words to be a mere enigma or parable, but might know that it is by all means needful to eat the Body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that eateth My flesh, dwelleth in Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This He said, showing that such an one is blended with Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now what follows seems unconnected, unless we enquire into the sense; for, saith some one, after saying, “He that eateth My flesh, dwelleth in Me,” what kind of a consequence is it to add,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “As the living Father hath sent Me, and I live by the Father”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet the words harmonize perfectly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since He continually spake of “eternal life,” to prove this point He introduceth the expression, “dwelleth in Me”; for “if he dwelleth in Me, and I live, it is plain that he will live also.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then He saith, “As the living Father hath sent Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is an expression of comparison and resemblance, and its meaning is of this kind, “I live in like manner as the Father liveth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And that thou mayest not deem Him unbegotten, He immediately subjoineth, “by the Father,” not by this to show that He needeth, in order to live, any power working in Him, for He said before, to remove such a suspicion, “As the Father hath life in Himself, so hath He given to the Son also to have life in Himself”; now if He needeth the working of another, it will be found that either the Father hath not given Him so to have it, and so the assertion is false, or if He hath so given it, then He will need no other one to support Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then means the, “By the Father”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He here merely hinteth at the cause, and what He saith is of this kind: “As the Father liveth, so I live, and he that eateth Me shall live by Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the “life” of which He speaketh is not life merely, but the excellent life; for that He spake not simply of life, but of that glorious and ineffable life, is clear from this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For all men “live,” even unbelievers, and uninitiated, who eat not of that flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that the words relate not to this life, but to that other?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what He saith is of this kind: “He that eateth My flesh, when he dieth shall not perish nor suffer punishment”; He spake not of the general resurrection, (for all alike rise again,) but concerning the special, the glorious Resurrection, that which hath a reward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “This is that bread which came down from heaven; not as your fathers did eat manna, and are dead; he that eateth of this bread shall live for ever.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Continually doth He handle the same point, so as to imprint it on the understanding of the hearers, (for the teaching on these points was a kind of final teaching,) and to confirm the doctrine of the Resurrection and of eternal life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He mentioneth the Resurrection since He promiseth eternal life, showing that that life is not now, but after the Resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And whence,” saith some one, “are these things clear?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From the Scriptures; to them He everywhere referreth the Jews, bidding them learn these things from them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And by saying, “Which giveth life to the world,” He inciteth them to jealousy, that from very vexation that others should enjoy the gift, they may not stay without.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And continually He remindeth them of the manna, showing the difference, (between it and His bread,) and guiding them to the faith; for if He was able to support their life for forty years without harvest, or corn, or other things in course; much more now will He be able to do so, as having come for greater ends.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, if those things were but types, and yet men collected what came down without sweat or labor; much more shall this be the case, where the difference is great both in the never dying, and in the enjoying the true life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And rightly hath He spoken often of “life,” since this is desired by men, and nothing is so pleasing to them as not to die.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since even under the old Covenant, this was the promise, length of life and many days, but now it is not length merely, but life having no end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He desireth at the same time to show, that He now revoketh the punishment caused by sin, annulling that sentence which condemneth to death, and bringing in not life merely, but life eternal, contrariwise to the former things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “These things said He in the synagogue, as He taught in Capernaum.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The place where most of His marvels had been done, so that He ought there especially to have been listened to.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But wherefore taught He in the synagogue and in the Temple?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As well because He desired to catch the greatest number of them, as because He desired to show that He was not opposed to the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But many of the disciples, when they had heard this, said, This is a hard saying.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What means “hard”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Rough, laborious, troublesome.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet He said nothing of this kind, for He spake not of a mode of life, but of doctrines, continually handling the faith which is in Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then means, “is a hard saying”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it because it promiseth life and resurrection?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it because He said that He came down from heaven?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or that it was impossible for one to be saved who ate not His flesh?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Tell me, are these things “hard”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Who can assert that they are?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then means “hard”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It means, “difficult to be received,” “transcending their infirmity,” “having much terror.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they thought that He uttered words too high for His real character, and such as were above Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore they said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps making excuse for themselves, since they were about to start away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “When Jesus knew in Himself that His disciples murmured at it,” (for this is an attribute of His Godhead to bring secret things to light,) “He said unto them, Doth this offend you?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What and if ye shall see the Son of Man ascend up where He was before?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This also He doth in the case of Nathanael, saying, “Because I said unto thee, I saw thee under the fig-tree, believest thou?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou shalt see greater things than these.” ( c. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And to Nicodemus, “No man hath ascended up to heaven but the Son of man which is in heaven.” ( c. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then, doth He add difficulties to difficulties?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: No, (that be far from Him,) but by the greatness of the doctrines, and the number of them, He desireth to bring them over.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if one had said simply, “I have come down from heaven,” and added nothing more, he would have been the more likely to offend them; but He who said, “My body is the life of the world”; He who said, “As the living Father hath sent Me, so I live by the Father”; and who said, “I have come down from heaven,” solves the difficulty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the man who utters any one great thing concerning himself may perhaps be suspected of feigning, but he who connects together so many one after another removes all suspicion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: All that He doth and saith is intended to lead them away from the thought, that Joseph was His father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And it was not with a wish to strengthen, but rather to do away that stumbling-block, that He said this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For whosoever deemed that He was Joseph’s son could not receive His sayings, while one that was persuaded that He had come down from heaven, and would ascend thither, might more easily give heed to His words: at the same time He bringeth forward also another explanation, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “It is the Spirit that quickeneth, the flesh profiteth nothing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: His meaning is, “Ye must hear spiritually what relateth to Me, for he who heareth carnally is not profited, nor gathereth any advantage.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was carnal to question how He came down from heaven, to deem that He was the son of Joseph, to ask, “How can he give us His flesh to eat?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: All this was carnal, when they ought to have understood the matter in a mystical and spiritual sense.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But,” saith some one, “how could they understand what the ‘eating flesh’ might mean?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then it was their duty to wait for the proper time and enquire, and not to abandon Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The words that I speak unto you, they are spirit and they are life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, they are divine and spiritual, have nothing carnal about them, are not subject to the laws of physical consequence, but are free from any such necessity, are even set above the laws appointed for this world, and have also another and a different meaning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now as in this passage He said “spirit,” instead of “spiritual,” so when He speaketh of “flesh,” He meant not “carnal things,” but “carnally hearing,” and alluding at the same time to them, because they ever desired carnal things when they ought to have desired spiritual.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if a man receives them carnally, he profits nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What then, is not His flesh, flesh?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Most certainly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How then saith He, that the flesh profiteth nothing?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He speaketh not of His own flesh, (God forbid!) but of those who received His words in a carnal manner.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what is “understanding carnally”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is looking merely to what is before our eyes, without imagining anything beyond.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is understanding carnally.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But we must not judge thus by sight, but must look into all mysteries with the eyes within.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is seeing spiritually.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He that eateth not His flesh, and drinketh not His blood, hath no life in him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then doth “the flesh profit nothing,” if without it we cannot live?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that the words, “the flesh profiteth nothing,” are spoken not of His own flesh, but of carnal hearing?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But there are some of you that believe not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, according to His custom, He addeth weight to His words, by foretelling what would come to pass, and by showing that He spake thus not from desire of honor from them, but because He cared for them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And when He said “some,” He excepted the disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For at first He said, “Ye have both seen Me, and believe not” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 36 ); but here, “There are some of you that believe not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He “knew from the beginning who they were that believed not, and who should betray Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And He said, Therefore said I unto you, that no man can come unto Me except it were given unto Him from above from My Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here the Evangelist intimates to us the voluntary character of the Dispensation, and His endurance of evil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nor is the, “from the beginning,” put here without a cause, but that thou mayest be aware of His foreknowledge from the first, and that before the words were uttered, and not after the men had murmured nor after they had been offended, He knew the traitor, but before, which was an attribute of Godhead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then He added, “Except it be given him from above from My Father”; thus persuading them to deem God His Father, not Joseph, and showing them that it is no common thing to believe in Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As though He had said, “Unbelievers disturb Me not; trouble Me not, astonish Me not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I know of old before they were created, I know to whom the Father hath given to believe;” and do thou, when thou hearest that “He hath given,” imagine not merely an arbitrary distribution, but that if any hath rendered himself worthy to receive the gift, he hath received it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “From that time many of His disciples went back, and walked no more with Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Rightly hath the Evangelist said, not that they “departed,” but that they “went back”; showing that they cut themselves off from any increase in virtue, and that by separating themselves they lost the faith which they had of old.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this was not the case with the twelve; wherefore He saith to them,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Will ye also go away?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again showing that He needeth not their ministry and service, and proving to them that it was not for this that He led them about with Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For how could He when He used such expressions even to them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why did He not praise them? why did He not approve them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Both because He preserved the dignity befitting a teacher, and also to show them that they ought rather to be attracted by this mode of dealing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For had He praised them, they might, supposing that they were doing Him a favor, have had some human feeling; but by showing them that He needed not their attendance, He kept them to Him the more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe with what prudence He spake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, “Depart ye,” (this would have been to thrust them from Him,) but asked them a question, “Will ye also go away?” the expression of one who would remove all force or compulsion, and who wished not that they should be attached to Him through any sense of shame, but with a sense of favor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By not openly accusing, but gently glancing at them, He showeth what is the truly wise course under such circumstances.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But we feel differently; with good reason, since we do everything holding fast our own honor, and therefore think that our estate is lowered by the departure of those who attend on us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He neither flattered nor repulsed them, but asked them a question.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now this was not the act of one despising them, but of one wishing them not to be restrained by force and compulsion: for to remain on such terms is the same as to depart.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith Peter?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “To whom shall we go?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast the words of eternal life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And we believe and are sure that Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that it was not the words that caused offense, but the heedlessness, and sloth, and wrong-mindedness of the hearers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For even had He not spoken, they would have been offended, and would not have ceased to be ever anxious about bodily food, ever nailed to earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, the disciples heard at the same time with the others, yet they declared an opinion contrary to theirs, saying, “To whom shall we go?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: An expression indicating much affection, for it shows that their Teacher was more precious to them than anything, than father or mother, or any possessions, and that if they withdrew from Him, they had not then whither to flee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then lest it should seem that he had said, “to whom shall we go?” because there were none that would receive them, he straightway added, “Thou hast the words of eternal life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the Jews listened carnally, and with human reasonings, but the disciples spiritually, and committing all to faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore Christ said, “The words which I have spoken unto you are spirit”; that is, “do not suppose that the teaching of My words is subject to the rule of material consequences, or to the necessity of created things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Things spiritual are not of this nature, nor endure to submit to the laws of earth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This also Paul declareth, saying, “Say not in thine heart, Who shall ascend into heaven? (that is, to bring Christ down;) or, Who shall descend into the deep? (that is, to bring up Christ again from the dead.”) ( Rom. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thou hast the words of eternal life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These men already admitted the Resurrection, and all the apportionment which shall be there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe the brotherly and affectionate man, how he maketh answer for all the band.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he said not, “I know,” but, “We know.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or rather, observe how he goes to the very words of his Teacher, not speaking as did the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They said, “This is the son of Joseph”; but he said, “Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God”; and “Thou hast the words of eternal life”; having perhaps heard Him say, “He that believeth on Me hath eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he showed that he retained all that had been said, by recalling the very words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then did Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He neither praised nor expressed admiration of Peter, though He had elsewhere done so; but what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Have not I chosen you twelve, and one of you is a devil?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since Peter said, “We believe,” Jesus excepteth Judas from the band.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In the other place Peter made no mention of the disciples; but when Christ said, “Whom say ye that I am?” he replied, “Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God” ( Matt. xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 15 ); but here, since he said, “We believe,” Christ with reason admitteth not Judas into that band.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this He did afar off, and long before the time, to check the wickedness of the traitor, knowing that He should avail nothing, yet doing His own part.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And remark His wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He made not the traitor manifest, yet allowed him not to be hidden; that on the one hand he might not lose all shame, and become more contentious; and on the other, that he might not, thinking to be unperceived, work his wicked deed without fear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore by degrees He bringeth plainer reproofs against him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: First, He numbered him too among the others, when He said, “There are some of you that believe not,” (for that He counted the traitor the Evangelist hath declared, saying, “For He knew from the beginning who they were that believed not, and who should betray Him;”) but when he yet remained such, He brought against him a more severe rebuke, “One of you is a devil,” yet made the fear common to them all, wishing to conceal him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And here it is worth while to enquire, why the disciples at this time said nothing, but afterwards were afraid and doubted, looking one upon another, and asking, “Lord, is it I?” ( Matt. xxvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 22 ), when Peter beckoned to John to find out the traitor, by enquiring of their Teacher which was he.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is the reason?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Peter had not yet heard, “Get thee behind me, Satan,” wherefore he had no fear at all; but when he had been rebuked, and though he spoke through strong affection, instead of being approved of, had even been called “Satan,” he afterwards with reason feared when he heard, “One of you shall betray Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, He saith not even now, “One of you shall betray Me,” but, “One of you is a devil”; wherefore they understood not what was spoken, but thought that He was only reflecting upon their wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But wherefore said He, “I have chosen you twelve, and one of you is a devil”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was to show that His teaching was entirely free from flattery.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that they might not think that He would flatter them, because when all had left Him they alone remained, and confessed by Peter that He was the Christ, He leadeth them away from such a suspicion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what He saith is of this kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Nothing abasheth Me from rebuking the bad; think not that because ye have remained I shall choose to flatter you, or that because ye have followed Me I shall not rebuke the wicked.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For neither doth another circumstance abash Me, which is much more powerful than this to abash a teacher.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he that remaineth affordeth a proof of his affection, while one that hath been chosen by a teacher, being rejected, attacheth to him a character for folly among senseless persons.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Still neither doth this cause Me to refrain from My reproofs.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This at least even now the heathen frigidly and senselessly urge against Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For God is not wont to make men good by compulsion and force, neither is His election and choice compulsory on those who are called, but persuasive.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And that thou mayest learn that the calling compelleth not, consider how many of these who have been called have come to perdition, so that it is clear that it lieth in our own will also to be saved, or to perish.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hearing therefore these things, learn we always to be sober and to watch.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if when he who was reckoned among that holy band, who had enjoyed so great a gift, who had wrought miracles, (for he too was with the others who were sent to raise the dead and to heal lepers,) if when he was seized by the dreadful disease of covetousness, and betrayed his Master, neither the favors, nor the gifts, nor the being with Christ, nor the attendance on Him, nor the washing the feet, nor the sharing His table, nor the bearing the bag, availed him, if these things rather served to help on his punishment, let us also fear lest we ever through covetousness imitate Judas.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou betrayest not Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when thou neglectest the poor man wasting with hunger, or perishing with cold, that man draws upon thee the same condemnation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When we partake of the Mysteries unworthily, we perish equally with the Christ-slayers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When we plunder, when we oppress those weaker than ourselves, we shall draw down upon us severest punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And with reason; for how long shall the love of things present so occupy us, superfluous as they are and unprofitable? since wealth consists in superfluities, in which no advantage is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How long shall we be nailed to vanities?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How long shall we not look through and away into heaven, not be sober, not be satiated with these fleeting things of earth, not learn by experience their worthlessness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us think of those who before us have been wealthy; are not all those things a dream? are they not a shadow, a flower? are they not a stream which floweth by? a story and a tale?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such a man has been rich, and where now is his wealth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It has gone, has perished, but the sins done by reason of it stay by him, and the punishment which is because of the sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yea, surely if there were no punishment, if no kingdom were set before us, it were a duty to show regard for those of like descent and family, to respect those who have like feelings with ourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But now we feed dogs, and many of us wild asses, and bears, and different beasts, while we care not for a man perishing with hunger; and a thing alien to us is more valued than that which is of our kin, and our own family less honored than creatures which are not so, nor related to us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it a fine thing to build one’s self splendid houses, to have many servants, to lie and gaze at a gilded roof?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then, assuredly, it is superfluous and unprofitable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For other buildings there are, far brighter and more majestic than these; on such we must gladden our eyes, for there is none to hinder us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wilt thou see the fairest of roofs?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At eventide look upon the starred heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But,” saith some one, “this roof is not mine.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet in truth this is more thine than that other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For thee it was made, and is common to thee and to thy brethren; the other is not thine, but theirs who after thy death inherit it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The one may do thee the greatest service, guiding thee by its beauty to its Creator; the other the greatest harm, becoming thy greatest accuser at the Day of Judgment, inasmuch as it is covered with gold, while Christ hath not even needful raiment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us not, I entreat you, be subject to such folly, let us not pursue things which flee away, and flee those which endure; let us not betray our own salvation, but hold fast to our hope of what shall be hereafter; the aged, as certainly knowing that but a little space of life is left us; the young, as well persuaded that what is left is not much.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that day cometh so as a thief in the night.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Knowing this, let wives exhort their husbands, and husbands admonish their wives; let us teach youths and maidens, and all instruct one another, to care not for present things, but to desire those which are to come, that we may be able also to obtain them; through the grace and loving-kindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom, to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, now and ever and world without end.
